summary: Reis challenges the conventional notion that career success means climbing the corporate ladder, arguing that many high-ranking executives feel unfulfilled despite their titles. He advocates for defining success on your own terms, whether through influence, helping others, or impact, rather than defaulting to the traditional path of title and prestige.
key_points: Traditional career success (title/rank) often leads to unfulfillment
Success can take many forms: title, prestige, influence, money, or helping others
The corporate executive route isn't the only valid path
Personal definition of success should guide career decisions
money_quotes: Based on the conversations (some might say therapy sessions) I have with many people in high-title/rank positions, the lamentation I hear is, 'Is this it? Is this what I worked hard to achieve?'
If this is the only path to success, we might need to rethink what success means.
The corporate executive route was never for me. Yet I'm having a lot of fun and consider myself 'successful.'
You have your path and definition of success. Go that way.
key_insight: Career success shouldn't be measured solely by title and rank—find your own definition of success and pursue that path instead of defaulting to conventional expectations.
